metrics.label: Jobs per $1M invested
metrics.source: LNG Canada: $40B investment, ~300 permanent jobs. Clean Energy Canada / Navius Research, A Pivotal Moment (2023)

values.clean-economy: 509,000 clean energy jobs in 2025, growing 7% annually to 2.7M by 2050

metrics.label: Local economic multiplier
metrics.source: IISD, Why Canadian LNG Is Not a Path to Energy Security (2025)

values.lng-expansion: Majority foreign-owned. Profits flow to Shell, PETRONAS, PetroChina, Mitsubishi, KOGAS.
values.clean-economy: Renewable hubs distributed across Canada, keeping jobs and investment in local communities

metrics.label: Future market growth
metrics.source: IEA Gas 2025; NRCan Clean Electricity Strategy (2024)

values.lng-expansion: IEA projects 65 bcm LNG surplus by 2030. Global gas demand peaks this decade.
values.clean-economy: Clean energy GDP projected to reach $107B with $58B in annual investment and 600,000+ jobs by 2030

metrics.label: Infrastructure lifespan
metrics.source: LNG Canada project documentation; IISD, LNG Expansion Canada Not Worth the Risk (2024)

values.lng-expansion: 25-40 year asset commitment. LNG Canada contracts run 27 years. High stranded asset risk.
values.clean-economy: Modular, upgradeable technology. Solar, wind, and storage costs continue to fall as capacity expands.

values.lng-expansion: CAD $3.93B in federal and BC government support committed to LNG by end of 2030
values.clean-economy: Subsidies decline as technology matures. Renewables already cheapest new power source in most markets.

metrics.label: Electricity rate impact
metrics.source: Canadian Climate Institute, Clean Electricity Affordable Energy (2023)

values.lng-expansion: LNG expansion strains BC grid capacity, increasing costs for households and industry
values.clean-economy: Average Canadian household energy spending projected to fall 12% by 2050 under clean electricity transition
